text.title: Customer Support Ops Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Customer Support Ops. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Build FAQ + KB BEFORE adding async channel. Without self-serve every ticket lands on the founder.
rule.rationale: Without self-serve front door, ticket volume scales linearly with revenue.

rule.statement: SLA tiered: Free 24-48h, Paid 4-24h, Enterprise 1-4h. Same SLA across tiers undercharges or under-serves.
rule.rationale: Single SLA either over-promises to Free or under-serves Enterprise.

rule.statement: ≥60% of repeat tickets resolved by canned replies. Below this, founder hours dominate.
rule.rationale: Below 60% canned coverage, founder support hours grow linearly.

rule.statement: Weekly review: top-5 ticket patterns feed FAQ + product backlog. Patterns that recur 3 weeks = product fix, not support fix.
rule.rationale: Repeat patterns ignored = recurring tickets forever.

rule.statement: Even solo: support owner is named on the spec. Future hire / co-founder slots in cleanly.
rule.rationale: Anonymous ownership breaks at first hire.

rule.statement: Canned replies are template-only; per-customer PII filled at send-time, never baked in.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ops-customer-support.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
